records.ety: [L. mimicus, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ mime: cf. F. mimique. See Mime.]

quotes: Oft, in her absence, mimic fancy wakes To imitate her.
Man is, of all creatures, the most mimical.

defs: Consisting of, or formed by, imitation; imitated; as, mimic gestures.
Imitative; characterized by resemblance to other forms; -- applied to crystals which by twinning resemble simple forms of a higher grade of symmetry.
notes: ☞ Mimic often implies something droll or ludicrous, and is less dignified than imitative.
